title: RSA Encryption, Signatures, and Safe Migration · Language Lens: Crypto Go Rsa Agility Migration
summary: A service encrypts large payloads directly with textbook RSA, verifies PKCS#1 v1.5 signatures by default, and accepts any PEM key without purpose or size validation. Predict the asset, adversary, security property, trust boundary, and falsifying evidence before opening the governing standard. Planned artifact: Add and verify the rsa agility migration increment in RSA-OAEP and RSA-PSS boundary adapter. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging…
objectives: Inventory RSA uses, migrate by purpose to modern approved alternatives, retain verified old-read windows, and remove obsolete private keys.
Recognize textbook RSA and PKCS#1 v1.5 encryption oracle risks and isolate unavoidable legacy interoperability.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Predict Crypto Go Rsa Agility Migration · Theory Crypto Go Rsa Agility Migration
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory cryptographic engineering team handles case 2412 during a replicated device restart. Build deterministic Go evidence to gate RSA on trusted keys, OAEP or PSS purpose, payload limits, and uniform failure; preserve accessible status, non-color meaning, keyboard operation, error recovery, and secret-safe diagnostics; then repeat or roll back a nonce, sequence, or key version and reject the stale state. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Crypto Go Rsa Agility Migration contract for the supplied data.
